2606.28548.title: Turn-Averaged SAEs for Feature Discovery and Long-Context Attribution
2606.28548.paper_summary: Sparse autoencoders (SAEs) have become a useful tool for extracting interpretable features in language models. However, standard SAE architectures operate on individual token activations, meaning that the number of active features scales linearly with context length, and studying long model transcripts becomes difficult. We introduce turn-averaged SAEs, which represent a single Human or Assistant turn with a fixed number of features by learning to reconstruct the average model activation across the turn. We find that turn-averaged features describe a single turn's high-level characteristics more completely than per-token features when judged by an LLM. We also demonstrate that turn-averaged SAEs greatly simplify common downstream uses of SAEs like attribution graphs. Broadly, turn-averaged SAEs make interpretability techniques practical at long context lengths.
2606.28548.paper_summary_zh: 稀疏自編碼器（SAEs）已成為在語言模型中提取可解釋特徵的有用工具。<lb/>然而，標準的 SAE 架構僅針對單個標記的激活進行操作，這意味著活躍特徵的數量隨著上下文長度線性增長，這使得研究長模型的轉錄變得困難。<lb/>我們引入了回合平均 SAE，通過學習重建整個回合的平均模型激活，將單個人類或助手的回合表示為固定數量的特徵。<lb/>我們發現，當由 LLM 評估時，回合平均特徵比每個標記特徵更全面地描述單個回合的高層次特徵。<lb/>我們還展示了回合平均 SAE 大大簡化了像歸因圖這樣的 SAE 常見下游應用。<lb/>總體而言，回合平均 SAE 使得在長上下文長度下的可解釋性技術變得實用。
2606.28548.author: Kevin Der et.al.
2606.28548.authors: Kevin Der, Harish Kamath, Ben Thompson
